data.title: As a code reviewer, I automatically evaluate test quality when diffs touch test files so bloat is caught regardless of origin
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a test quality review overlay to the code review pipeline, triggered when the diff modifies test files. The overlay uses opus to detect bloat patterns: change-detector tests, implementation-coupled assertions, tautological tests, source-file-grepping, existence-only assertions. Simultaneously remove the redundant sprint-level test review step.
**Why**: Currently only sprint-level test review catches test bloat, and only for tests written during sprint execution. Tests from fix-bug, review resolution, or manual commits are unreviewed. A pipeline-level overlay catches bloat regardless of origin.
**Scope**:
- IN: New test_quality_overlay flag in the review complexity classifier, triggered by test file changes in the diff
- IN: New test quality review agent (opus) with checklist derived from the behavioral testing standard
- IN: Integration into the review workflow (modeled on security/performance overlay pattern)
- IN: Removal of the redundant sprint-level test review step (coordinated to prevent concurrent conflicting mechanisms)
- OUT: Pre-commit static analysis gate (separate story)
- OUT: The shared behavioral testing standard itself (epic 8df3-61d0)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the code review pipeline automatically triggers a test quality overlay when the diff modifies test files, detecting bloat patterns
  ← Satisfies: "A test quality review overlay is added to the code review pipeline"
- When this story is complete, the redundant test review step in the sprint skill is removed, replaced by the test quality review overlay
  ← Satisfies: "The redundant test review step in the sprint skill is removed, replaced by the test quality review overlay"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Overlay removal and sprint review removal must be atomic — no gap or overlap in test review coverage
- [Maintainability] New overlay should follow existing security/performance overlay pattern (classifier, agent, workflow)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
